2606.07300.title: Phun-Bench: Evaluating LLMs on Phonological Understanding in Chinese
2606.07300.paper_summary: Language is a vehicle for thought, intricately tied to sounds, symbols, and meaning. However, most large language model (LLM) research focuses on meaning (semantics) and symbols (spelling) while largely overlooking sounds. Existing benchmarks on LLMs' phonological abilities are either solvable through rote memorization or intertwined with other abilities, making them inadequate to measure LLMs' genuine ability in phonological understanding. Here, we present Phun-Bench, a purpose-built Chinese benchmark with diverse tasks and settings across three dimensions (Homophony, Rhyme, and Phonetic Similarity), designed to systematically evaluate LLMs' phonological understanding. Our results show that while LLMs excel at recalling correct pronunciations, they generally struggle to leverage phonological knowledge in the flexible and intuitive way that human speakers do. Moreover, through detailed analyses, we propose a hypothesis regarding the underlying mechanism of LLMs' phonological understanding and "perception", highlighting an underexplored frontier for future research.
2606.07300.paper_summary_zh: 語言是思想的載體，與聲音、符號和意義密切相關。<lb/>然而，大多數大型語言模型（LLM）研究集中於意義（語義）和符號（拼寫），而在很大程度上忽視了聲音。<lb/>現有的 LLM 音韻能力基準要麼可以通過死記硬背來解決，要麼與其他能力交織在一起，使其不足以衡量 LLM 在音韻理解方面的真正能力。<lb/>在此，我們提出了 Phun-Bench，一個專門設計的中文基準，涵蓋三個維度（同音、韻律和音素相似性）的多樣任務和設置，旨在系統性地評估 LLM 的音韻理解。<lb/>我們的結果顯示，儘管 LLM 在回憶正確發音方面表現出色，但它們通常難以像人類說話者那樣靈活和直觀地利用音韻知識。<lb/>此外，通過詳細分析，我們提出了一個關於 LLM 音韻理解和“感知”的潛在機制的假設，突顯了未來研究的一個尚未充分探索的前沿。
2606.07300.author: Xing Yue et.al.
2606.07300.authors: Xing Yue, Yongliang Shen, Weiming Lu
